steps.title: Read the context
steps.content: The father is at the station 'trying to find me among some other people' after the narrator's eleven-year absence. He's searching to spot the narrator in the crowd — a physical search, not a discovery of meaning.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The word "find" needs to mean spotting someone by sight in a crowd. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Locate: trying to locate me among some other people — locate means to find the position of someone, exactly right for scanning a crowd to spot a face. ✓
  • Choice B — Reveal: trying to reveal me among some other people — reveal means to make something known or visible; a father cannot reveal his own son to himself by looking. ✗
  • Choice C — Persuade: trying to persuade me among some other people — persuade means to convince someone of something; the father is searching visually, not arguing. ✗
  • Choice D — Embarrass: trying to embarrass me among some other people — embarrass means to cause discomfort; nothing in the passage suggests the father's intent is to humiliate the narrator. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
